title: torilis helvetica var. purpurea
intro: Torilis helvetica var. purpurea, also known as the Purple Hedge Parsley, is a biennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the relief of menstrual cramps, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Torilis helvetica var. purpurea can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are made by steeping the plant's leaves and roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
